terms.definition: This phenomenon occurs when an LLM bypasses its own high-quality internal training data to trigger external tools, such as calculators or search APIs, for tasks it could have solved natively. It represents a misalignment in the model's orchestration logic where the overhead of external execution is incurred unnecessarily, often leading to increased latency and redundant processing.
terms.analogy: It is like a professional chef who insists on driving to the store to buy pre-chopped onions for every single dish, even though they have a bag of fresh onions and a sharp knife sitting right in front of them.
terms.business_value: Over-reliance on external tools inflates API costs and introduces unnecessary points of failure. Executives must ensure their AI architects optimize the 'routing logic' to balance internal reasoning with external tool-use to maintain high margins and low latency.

terms.definition: IHR is a dimensionless diagnostic metric used to quantify the stability of an AI system operating under resource or time constraints. It calculates the ratio between a system's total inferential capacity and the level of environmental uncertainty it must resolve, serving as a 'stress test' indicator for autonomous decision-making systems.
terms.analogy: Think of IHR as the 'redline' on a car's tachometer; it tells you exactly how much more power the engine has available to climb a steep hill before the vehicle stalls under the weight of the climb.
terms.business_value: For mission-critical AI, IHR provides a mathematical safeguard against system collapse during high-volatility events. It allows leadership to move from 'guessing' system reliability to 'measuring' the exact buffer required to prevent catastrophic service interruptions.

terms.definition: This paradigm shifts AI development from simply 'weight tuning' within a fixed model to 'architectural discovery.' It uses open-ended evolutionary algorithms to automatically design the actual structure of the mathematical operations, allowing the AI to invent new, more efficient logic flows that human engineers might never have considered.
terms.analogy: Instead of just trying to find the best settings on a standard kitchen stove, you allow an automated system to completely reconfigure the kitchen—moving the sink, adding new heat sources, and rearranging the workflow—to find the most efficient way to produce a meal.
terms.business_value: This approach reduces the R&D bottleneck caused by a shortage of elite machine learning engineers. By automating the discovery of model structures, companies can create leaner, proprietary 'EvoForests' that outperform massive, generic models at a fraction of the operational compute cost.
